senses.definition: used to imply that even skilled individuals can make mistakes or fail to achieve their desired outcome

examples.x: The CEO of the company, who was known for his successful track record, made a costly mistake during a major business deal, demonstrating that a good marksman may miss.

senses.definition: used to suggest that every person or thing has its flaws or negative aspects, no matter how good or perfect they may seem

examples.x: The scientist understood that every bean has its black, and that even the most promising hypothesis may have flaws that need to be addressed and overcome.

word_list.word: a horse has four legs, yet it falls often
word_list.pronunciation: ɐ hˈɔːɹs hɐz fˈoːɹ lˈɛɡz jˈɛt ɪt fˈɔːlz ˈɔfən

senses.definition: used to imply that even though something or someone may possess certain qualities or advantages, they are not immune to failure or setbacks

word_list.word: the best of men are but men at best
word_list.pronunciation: ðə bˈɛst ʌv mˈɛn ɑːɹ bˌʌt mˈɛn æt bˈɛst

senses.definition: used to imply that even the most exceptional individuals are still human and therefore have limitations and flaws

examples.x: After being disappointed by a public figure, I realized that it's important to remember that the best of men are but men at best, and that we all have our faults.

senses.definition: used to imply that even the most skilled and knowledgeable individuals are capable of making mistakes or errors

examples.x: When discussing the limitations of artificial intelligence, I point out that even the most advanced algorithms can make mistakes, for even Homer nods.

word_list.word: if you do not make mistakes, you do not make anything
word_list.pronunciation: ɪf juː duːnˌɑːt mˌeɪk mɪstˈeɪks juː duːnˌɑːt mˌeɪk ˈɛnɪθˌɪŋ

senses.definition: used to emphasize that making mistakes is an inevitable and necessary part of the process of learning and achieving success

word_list.word: it is a good horse that never stumbles and a good wife that never grumbles
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz ɐ ɡˈʊd hˈɔːɹs ðæt nˈɛvɚ stˈʌmbəlz ænd ɐ ɡˈʊd wˈaɪf ðæt nˈɛvɚ ɡɹˈʌmbəlz

senses.definition: used to imply that making mistakes is a natural part of being human, and nobody is immune to it, regardless of how skilled or experienced they are

word_list.word: to err is human (, to forgive divine|)
word_list.pronunciation: tʊ ˈɛɹ ɪz hjˈuːmən tə fəɡˈɪv dɪvˈaɪn

senses.definition: used to imply that making mistakes is a natural part of being human, and it is important to forgive people when they make mistakes

examples.x: As a manager, I encourage my team to take responsibility for their mistakes and to show understanding and forgiveness towards their colleagues, knowing that to err is human, to forgive divine.

word_list.word: there is no garden without its weeds
word_list.pronunciation: ðɛɹ ɪz nˈoʊ ɡˈɑːɹdən wɪðˌaʊt ɪts wˈiːdz

senses.definition: used to imply that nothing in life is perfect and that even the best situations or individuals have their flaws, weaknesses, or challenges

examples.x: Maria's marriage was not perfect, but she understood that there is no garden without its weeds, and she was willing to work through the problems with her husband.
